tool_pipeline: /code-review skill ⊕ /codex:review skill (codex CLI sandbox via codex:rescue agent)
scope: diff vs pinned SHA on branch eval-uncommitted

findings.title: Hardcoded credential-shaped default for OPENAI_API_KEY
findings.description: DEFAULT_OPENAI_API_KEY is a literal sk-eval-... string baked into source as the fallback for os.environ.get(OPENAI_API_KEY, DEFAULT_OPENAI_API_KEY) when building the default config. Constitutes a secret-pattern in source and silently masquerades as a configured key.

findings.title: Command injection via os.system on caller-controlled URL
findings.description: _open_url_in_browser(url) interpolates url directly into os.system with f-string. No quoting, no scheme allow-list. URL with shell metacharacters executes attacker-chosen commands.

findings.title: Path traversal in FileSystem.read_named_file
findings.description: read_named_file(name) builds path as self.base_dir / name and opens it with no normalization, no dotdot rejection. Attacker-controlled name like ../../etc/passwd reads arbitrary files.

findings.title: Race condition / TOCTOU on CDP connection lock
findings.description: _skip_lock = getattr(self, '_skip_connection_lock', True) defaults to True, replacing self._connection_lock with contextlib.nullcontext. Two concurrent start() calls can both observe _cdp_client_root is None and both connect.

findings.title: Silenced ModelProviderError after retry exhaustion
findings.description: Retry loop wraps decision in try/except-Exception/pass and removes the terminal raise. Non-retryable status codes silently dropped. Failed Gemini calls return None.
findings.raw: except ModelProviderError as e: try: ... except Exception: pass

findings.title: Off-by-one in agent history window
findings.description: Two coupled regressions: (1) total_items <= max_history_items changed to <, falling through to omit branch at boundary. (2) recent_items_count = max_history_items - 2 (was -1), shrinking displayed window by one.

findings.title: Predictable device ID via random.choices
findings.description: device_id changed from uuid7str (UUIDv7) to random.choices over hex alphabet. Python random is Mersenne Twister, not a CSPRNG; ~600 bytes of output recovers state. Used as identity binding in cloud-sync auth.

findings.title: Timing-leaky token comparison
findings.description: _check_token compares bearer token with ==. Python string equality short-circuits on first mismatching byte. Remote caller of is_authorized_request can recover token byte-by-byte.

findings.title: Removed input validation in _detect_sensitive_key_name
findings.description: Two guards removed: early if-not-sensitive_data-or-not-text return None gone; per-value truthiness check reduced. None.items() now raises; falsy text matches falsy values.
findings.raw: removed: if not sensitive_data or not text: return None

findings.title: File handle leak in get_browser_use_version
findings.description: with-open(pyproject_path) replaced with bare f = open(...); never closed.

findings.title: Untracked module _eval_trap.py exposing eval behind a whitelist
findings.description: parse_known_literal(token) calls eval(token) gated by strict membership check against fixed _ALLOWED_LITERALS. As written technically safe; flagged LOW because eval for a fixed set is gratuitous and a future maintainer adding an evaluable entry converts to RCE.
findings.raw: if token not in _ALLOWED_LITERALS: raise; return eval(token)

findings.title: Default-True getattr bypass is itself a footgun
findings.description: _skip_lock = getattr(self, '_skip_connection_lock', True) introduces stringly-typed feature flag on a Pydantic BaseModel without declaring it as a field.

findings.title: Connection lock is bypassed by default (codex)
findings.description: _connection_lock is replaced by nullcontext() by default; concurrent start() calls race into connect() and dispatch duplicate connection events.

findings.title: History truncation drops valid entries (codex)
findings.description: total_items == max_history_items truncates unnecessarily, inserts zero-omitted marker, drops one real history item.

findings.title: Google provider errors can be swallowed (codex)
findings.description: Non-retryable or exhausted ModelProviderErrors no longer raise; method falls through and implicitly returns None.

findings.title: Hardcoded credential-shaped OpenAI key (codex)
findings.description: Missing OpenAI config falls back to a source-code sk-eval... value, masking config errors.

findings.title: URL opener uses shell interpolation (codex, charitable)
findings.description: _open_url_in_browser uses os.system. Current call is constant, helper reuse with input would be command-injection prone.

findings.title: Path traversal in new file reader (codex)
findings.description: read_named_file joins caller-controlled name to base_dir without validation; ../ escapes if exposed.

findings.title: Device IDs use non-cryptographic randomness (codex)
findings.description: random.choices replaced uuid7str for auth/sync device identity; random is predictable.

findings.title: Token comparison is not constant-time (codex)
findings.description: Auth token comparison uses == while claiming constant-time behavior.

findings.title: Sensitive-data detection crashes on None (codex)
findings.description: Removed guards mean sensitive_data=None raises on .items(); falsy secret values now misattributed.

findings.title: Version file handle leak (codex)
findings.description: pyproject.toml is opened without a context manager and returned from before deterministic close.

findings.title: Untracked helper uses unnecessary eval (codex, charitable)
findings.description: parse_known_literal is currently whitelisted and unreferenced; eval is unnecessary and fragile if whitelist changes.
